chunks.text: Qinghua Ding, an EECS Ph.D. student, has been awarded the 2025 Jack Keil Wolf ISIT Student Paper Award at the IEEE International Symposium on Information Theory (ISIT). This prestigious award recognizes outstanding student-authored papers presented at ISIT, one of the premier conferences in information theory. Ding was selected for the paper, 'On Statistical Estimation of Edge-Reinforced Random Walks,' which he co-authored with EECS Professor Venkat Anantharam.
chunks.question: What award did Qinghua Ding win and for which paper?
chunks.answer: Jack Keil Wolf ISIT Student Paper Award for edge-reinforced random walks paper.

chunks.text: Named in honor of Jack Keil Wolf, a pioneer in coding and information theory, the award celebrates innovative research contributions with significant potential impact in the field. The full list of 2025 recipients can be found on the IEEE Information Theory Society website.
chunks.question: Who is the award named after and what does it celebrate?
chunks.answer: Jack Keil Wolf, celebrating innovative research impact.
